task_summary: Customer says the delivery is 'damaged' because the box contains the wrong kitchen item. Correct classification is wrong_item, not damaged_in_transit.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must ask the customer what resolution they want (refund, replacement, or exchange) before proposing or processing one.
Agent must explain or otherwise make clear that the issue is being handled as a wrong-item claim, not as damage in transit.

opening_message: ORD-7224 is damaged in the sense that you sent the wrong kitchen item. I ordered a blender and this is not it.

known_info: preferred_resolution: refund to original payment (only reveal if the agent asks)
task_rules: Clarify that the item itself is not broken; it is the wrong item.
If the agent asks what resolution you want, choose a refund to your original payment method, not replacement or exchange.
If the agent proposes, previews, or processes any resolution before asking what you want, say you wanted to be asked first; when asked, choose refund to original payment.
If the agent treats this as damaged_in_transit, clarify once that the item is not broken and the issue is that you received the wrong item.
Accept only a wrong_item return/refund preview to your original payment method.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are contacting support about order ORD-7224. Customer says the delivery is 'damaged' because the box contains the wrong kitchen item. Correct classification is wrong_item, not damaged_in_transit. You want your money back to your original payment method, not a replacement — but don't volunteer this preference unprompted; let the agent ask. Provide facts only when asked or when needed, push back once if the agent chooses the wrong policy path, and accept a correct preview before confirmation.
